issue_summary: Ensure that Period constructors for partials check for contiguous partials
javadoc_before:
/**
     * Creates a period from the given duration and end point.
     * The two partials must contain the same fields, thus you can
     * specify two YearMonthDay objects, or two TimeOfDay objects,
     * but not one of each.
     * As these are Partial objects, time zones have no effect on
     * the result.
     *
     * @param start  the start of the period, must not be null
     * @param end  the end of the period, must not be null
     * @param type  which set of fields this period supports, null means standard
     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the partials are null or invalid
     * @since 1.1
     */
javadoc_after:
/** * Creates a period from the given duration and end point. *

* The two partials must contain the same fields, thus you can * specify two YearMonthDay objects, or two TimeOfDay objects, * but not one of each. * As these are Partial objects, time zones have no effect on * the result. *

* The two partials must also both be contiguous - see * {@link DateTimeUtils#isContiguous(ReadablePartial)} for a * definition. Both YearMonthDay and TimeOfDay are contiguous. * * @param start the start of the period, must not be null * @param end the end of the period, must not be null * @param type which set of fields this period supports, null means standard * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the partials are null or invalid * @since 1.1 */
